articles.title: Hybrid Work, Focus Hours, and the Modern Office
articles.content: Hybrid work has changed how teams use their office days. In this article, we explore how focus hours, collaboration rituals, and quiet zones can coexist in a modern hybrid office. We break down scheduling patterns, zoning strategies, and acoustic tips that help teams stay productive when only part of the team is on-site.
articles.excerpt: Learn how to blend focus hours and collaboration rituals in a hybrid office without sacrificing productivity.

articles.title: A One-Week Refresh Plan for Hybrid Neighborhoods
articles.content: Not every office update needs to be a full renovation. This one-week refresh plan focuses on small, high-impact changes across hybrid neighborhoods: updating task chairs, adding mobile whiteboards, and re-zoning underused corners into call booths or touchdown areas.
articles.excerpt: Use this 7-day checklist to quickly refresh hybrid workspace neighborhoods without a major renovation.

articles.title: Hybrid Work Onboarding Checklist for New Hires
articles.content: Onboarding in a hybrid environment requires clarity around where work happens. This checklist covers technology, workspace norms, desk booking, and etiquette for using quiet rooms and collaboration zones.
articles.excerpt: Give new hybrid employees a clear roadmap for using your office effectively from day one.

articles.title: Designing for Neurodiversity in Open-Plan Offices
articles.content: Open-plan offices can be challenging for neurodivergent employees. We explore zoning, acoustic partitions, lighting control, and retreat spaces that support a wider range of sensory needs.
articles.excerpt: Practical design moves to make open-plan offices more inclusive for neurodivergent teammates.

articles.title: Ergonomic Chair Basics: 5 Adjustments That Matter Most
articles.content: Seat height, depth, lumbar support, armrests, and recline tension are the five adjustments that drive most day-to-day comfort. We explain how to set up each one for different body types.
articles.excerpt: Before you invest in new chairs, understand the five adjustments that deliver the biggest comfort gains.

articles.title: Workspace Trends 2026: Flexible, Measured, and Human
articles.content: From sensor-informed utilization to softer, more residential finishes, 2026 continues the shift toward spaces that flex with teams and prioritize human experience.
articles.excerpt: Our annual look at the workspace trends reshaping offices in 2026.

articles.title: Why Change Management Workshops Belong in Your Office Redesign
articles.content: New layouts only succeed when people know how to use them. Change management workshops help teams practice new behaviors, from booking focus rooms to using shared project tables.
articles.excerpt: Space alone won’t change behavior—here’s how workshops bridge the gap.

articles.title: Designing a 15-Person Startup Office on a Tight Budget
articles.content: We break down one of our favorite lean startup offices and share the budget, product mix, and layout decisions that made it work.
articles.excerpt: See how a 15-person team built a flexible office without overspending.

articles.title: What to Prepare for Your Virtual Office Design Consultation
articles.content: Floor plans, headcount assumptions, photos, and culture notes all help us make the most of a 60-minute virtual consultation.
articles.excerpt: A short checklist to get the most value from your first virtual design session.

articles.title: Hybrid-Ready Meeting Rooms in Under 20 Square Meters
articles.content: Small rooms can still host great hybrid meetings. We cover display positioning, camera angles, acoustics, and furniture layouts for compact, hybrid-ready rooms.
articles.excerpt: Turn small rooms into inclusive, hybrid-ready collaboration spaces.

articles.title: How Our Office Space Calculator Works
articles.content: We walk through the assumptions behind our calculator: occupancy rates, collaboration ratios, and amenity space factors for hybrid, remote-first, and office-first teams.
articles.excerpt: Understand the math behind your space recommendations.

articles.title: From Quiz Results to Real Offices: Making Your Style Work
articles.content: Your style quiz results are a starting point. We show how to translate a modern-leaning style profile into actual furniture, colors, and layouts.
articles.excerpt: Turn your digital moodboard into a real, coherent office.

consultation_time_slots.notes: On-site visits limited to city center

portfolio_projects.name: Scrappy Suite: 15-Person Startup Loft
portfolio_projects.short_description: Lean, flexible loft office for a 15-person SaaS startup, delivered on a tight budget.
portfolio_projects.full_description: This project transformed a compact loft into a flexible startup office with bench desks, a single enclosed focus room, and a soft seating zone that doubles as an all-hands space. The design prioritized cost-effective furniture and a restrained material palette.

portfolio_projects.name: Bright Garage Loft for Growing Team
portfolio_projects.short_description: Converted garage loft for a 12-person product team with room to grow.
portfolio_projects.full_description: Originally a car workshop, this space now houses a growing startup with flexible bench desks, project tables, and a mezzanine lounge. Low-cost finishes and simple lighting kept the budget tight while delivering character.

portfolio_projects.name: Micro Studio for 8-Person Founding Team
portfolio_projects.short_description: Ultra-compact studio office optimized for an 8-person founding team.
portfolio_projects.full_description: A tiny footprint required smart zoning and furniture. Wall-mounted storage, slim desks, and a foldable collaboration table keep circulation clear while supporting daily standups and focus work.

portfolio_projects.name: Hybrid Headquarters for 80
portfolio_projects.short_description: New hybrid HQ with neighborhoods, focus rooms, and social hub for an 80-person fintech.
portfolio_projects.full_description: We created activity-based neighborhoods aligned to teams, a large social hub, and a suite of video-first rooms to support hybrid collaboration. Sensor data informed space allocation between focus, collaboration, and amenity zones.

portfolio_projects.name: Corporate HQ with Client Experience Center
portfolio_projects.short_description: 120-person HQ with dedicated client journey from lobby to showcase spaces.
portfolio_projects.full_description: This HQ combines employee neighborhoods with a client experience center, blending hospitality-inspired finishes with robust workspaces.

portfolio_projects.name: 20-Person Satellite Office with Hot Desks
portfolio_projects.short_description: Compact satellite office with bookable hot desks and two focus rooms.
portfolio_projects.full_description: Designed for regional sales and support staff, this satellite office emphasizes booking flexibility and fast-touch collaboration.

portfolio_projects.name: Open-Plan Hub for 50 Engineers
portfolio_projects.short_description: Engineers-first open-plan office with adjacent quiet rooms.
portfolio_projects.full_description: This open-plan office clusters engineers near quiet rooms and project tables, supporting both deep focus and peer code reviews.

portfolio_projects.name: Creative Coworking Loft
portfolio_projects.short_description: Lush coworking loft with layered social and focus zones for creatives.
portfolio_projects.full_description: A balance of large shared tables, maker benches, and quiet nooks supports a diverse creative community.

portfolio_projects.name: Executive Suite with Terrace
portfolio_projects.short_description: Executive suite with private terraces and client-ready meeting rooms.
portfolio_projects.full_description: Warm materials, acoustic treatments, and tailored furniture create a calm executive environment overlooking the city.

portfolio_projects.name: Light Refresh for 10-Person Satellite
portfolio_projects.short_description: Furniture and finish refresh for a compact satellite office.
portfolio_projects.full_description: Updated task chairs, acoustic panels, and new paint dramatically improved comfort and focus, without changing the floor plan.

portfolio_projects.name: Hybrid Neighborhoods for 40
portfolio_projects.short_description: Hybrid neighborhoods with a mix of focus pods and collaboration tables.
portfolio_projects.full_description: Designed for a 40-person product team, this office uses three distinct neighborhoods tuned for different work modes.

portfolio_projects.name: Boutique Coworking for Startups
portfolio_projects.short_description: Compact coworking hub tailored to early-stage startups.
portfolio_projects.full_description: An intimate coworking space with shared bench desks, quiet booths, and a cozy cafe corner.

style_quiz_questions.text: How do you want your team to feel when they walk into the office?
Which furniture silhouette feels most like your brand?
Pick the mix of materials you prefer.
How formal or casual should your workspace feel?
Which lighting mood best matches your ideal office?
How much color do you want in your main work areas?
Which breakout space would your team use most?
What best describes your company’s brand personality?
How important is it that your office feels distinctly "Modern" versus "Classic"?
Which image looks closest to your ideal meeting space?

style_profiles.name: Modern Warm Workspace
style_profiles.description: Clean lines, warm woods, and soft textiles that feel current without being cold. Ideal for hybrid tech and creative teams who want a polished but welcoming office.

style_profiles.name: Modern Minimal Focus
style_profiles.description: Highly streamlined and clutter-free, with monochrome palettes and simple geometry to support deep focus and clarity.

style_profiles.name: Industrial Loft Studio
style_profiles.description: Exposed structure, raw metals, and statement lighting with modern furniture forms. Best for brands that want a creative, urban edge.

style_profiles.name: Scandinavian Calm & Bright
style_profiles.description: Light woods, soft neutrals, and plants with a relaxed, home-like feeling. Great for companies that value wellbeing and simplicity.

style_profiles.name: Classic Refined Workspace
style_profiles.description: Layered textures, structured layouts, and timeless furniture pieces that communicate stability and heritage.

style_profiles.name: Eclectic Playful Studio
style_profiles.description: Bold color, mixed furniture styles, and unexpected details that showcase personality and creativity.

style_quiz_answer_options.text: Energised and creative, with a gentle buzz of activity.
Calm, ordered, and quietly focused.

More substantial, classic pieces with rounded edges.
Light wood, white surfaces, and soft textiles.
Darker woods, metals, and richer textures.
Relaxed and informal, with flexible seating.
More structured and formal, with clear zones.
Bright, evenly lit spaces with plenty of daylight.
More dramatic, with focused pools of light and contrast.
Mostly neutrals with small colour accents.
Bold colour moments that stand out.
A cafe-like social space with soft seating.
A quiet library-style space with focus booths.
Innovative, bold, and future-focused.
Established, trusted, and timeless.
Strongly modern I want the office to feel current and forward-looking.
More classic, with only subtle modern touches.
A glass-walled, tech-enabled room with simple furnishings.
